perturbation.original_text: Vector understands and acknowledges that, except pursuant to a final definitive agreement regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, (i) neither Captaris nor any of’ its Representatives make any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material, (ii) neither Captaris nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Vector or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, and (iii) only those representations or warranties which are made in any such final definitive agreement will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Vector understands and acknowledges that, except pursuant to a final definitive agreement regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, (i) Captaris makes representations and warranties, express and implied, as to the accuracy and completeness of the Evaluation Material, (ii) neither Captaris nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Vector or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, and (iii) only those representations or warranties which are made in any such final definitive agreement will have any legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by directly stating that Captaris makes representations and warranties regarding the evaluation material, whereas the original text explicitly stated that Captaris *does not* make such representations or warranties. This introduces contradiction and legal uncertainty regarding the reliability of the Evaluation Material and the potential liability of Captaris if the material is inaccurate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vector understands and acknowledges that, except pursuant to a final definitive agreement regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, (i) Captaris makes representations and warranties, express and implied, as to the accuracy and completeness of the Evaluation Material, (ii) neither Captaris nor its Representatives shall have any liability to Vector or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, and (iii) only those representations or warranties which are made in any such final definitive agreement will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text stated that Captaris does *not* make representations or warranties about the evaluation material. The changed text states that Captaris *does* make such representations. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which also states Captaris makes such representations/warranties, creating a clear inconsistency and impacting the enforceability of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Each party further acknowledges and agrees that the other party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals with regard to a transaction between the parties, and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party further acknowledges and agrees that the other party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals with regard to a transaction between the parties, but must provide 30 days written notice before terminating discussions and negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the termination of discussions. The original text allows either party to terminate discussions at any time, in their sole discretion. The change adds a requirement of 30 days written notice before terminating, which contradicts the 'at any time' clause, createing uncertainties regarding terms of discussion termination.
perturbation.location: Paragraph regarding Right to Reject Proposals
perturbation.contradicted_location: Paragraph regarding Right to Reject Proposals
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party further acknowledges and agrees that the other party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals with regard to a transaction between the parties, but must provide 30 days written notice before terminating discussions and negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that either party can terminate discussions at any time. The changed text adds a requirement of 30 days written notice before terminating discussions and negotiations. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now includes this 30-day notice requirement, which directly contradicts the 'at any time' clause implied in the original text, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty regarding the terms of discussion termination, fitting the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this letter agreement by either party or any of its Representatives and that the parties shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this letter agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this letter agreement by either party or any of its Representatives and that the parties shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this letter agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that money damages would *not* be a sufficient remedy. The modified text contradicts this statement by claiming that money damages *would* be a sufficient remedy. This introduces contradiction and uncertainty regarding the appropriate remedies for a breach, making it unclear whether a party can only seek monetary compensation or also equitable relief. This directly creates in-text contradiction, on the remedies that can be pursue in a case of breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this letter agreement by either party or any of its Representatives and that the parties shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this letter agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that money damages would *not* be a sufficient remedy. The changed text states the opposite, claiming that money damages *would* be a sufficient remedy. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this directly contradicts the original intention and creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the availability and sufficiency of monetary damages, which falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished by Captaris hereunder, Vector hereby agrees that, for a period of twelve months from the date hereof, it will not, and will not encourage or assist its affiliates to, solicit for employment (a) any officers or other senior key employees of Captaris or any of its business units so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris, and (b) any of the other employees of Captaris or any of its business units with whom Vector has had contact or who Vector first became aware of during the period of its investigation of Captaris, so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished by Captaris hereunder, Vector hereby agrees that, for a period of six months from the date hereof, it will not, and will not encourage or assist its affiliates to, solicit for employment (a) any officers or other senior key employees of Captaris or any of its business units so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris, and (b) any of the other employees of Captaris or any of its business units with whom Vector has had contact or who Vector first became aware of during the period of its investigation of Captaris, so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris.
perturbation.explanation: The change in the non-solicitation period from twelve months to six months creates an in-text contradiction. The original agreement restricts Vector from soliciting employees for twelve months, but after the modification, it's only six months. This causes uncertainty and legal ambiguity regarding the valid duration of the non-solicitation agreement. The period for such agreement is directly in-text contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished by Captaris hereunder, Vector hereby agrees that, for a period of six months from the date hereof, it will not, and will not encourage or assist its affiliates to, solicit for employment (a) any officers or other senior key employees of Captaris or any of its business units so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris, and (b) any of the other employees of Captaris or any of its business units with whom Vector has had contact or who Vector first became aware of during the period of its investigation of Captaris, so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a twelve-month non-solicitation period, while the changed text reduces it to six months. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also specifies a six-month period. The change creates a clear inconsistency regarding the valid duration of the non-solicitation agreement within the contract, aligning with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: This letter agreement will terminate on the second anniversary of the date of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This letter agreement will terminate on the first anniversary of the date of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states a two-year term for the agreement. The modified text now states a one-year term. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the duration of the agreement, leading to uncertainty about when the agreement expires and ceases to be legally binding. This directly contradicts in-text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This letter agreement will terminate on the first anniversary of the date of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a termination date two years from the agreement date. The changed text modifies this to one year. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the agreement terminates on the first anniversary, causing a clear inconsistency regarding the agreement's duration. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines' creating a strong contradiction.
